name: Divided block
description: The divided block is a content unit that represents a single step, stage, or content item. It supports a wide range of content types, including text, [Image](/global/construct/image), videos, [List](/global/construct/list), [Code](/global/construct/code), [Logo block](/sites/construct/logo-block), [Notification](/global/construct/notification), and [CTA block](/sites/construct/cta-block). This block is used as the primary content unit in the [Divided section](/sites/construct/divided-section).
usage:
Use Divided block to present multiple related items within a single section while keeping each item clearly separated. It works best when each item may require a different combination of content elements.  
Divided block is well-suited for step-based content such as tutorials, setup guides, onboarding flows, or implementation processes. It also works for presenting multiple items within a single topic — such as stages in a workflow, feature explanations, or grouped content items — where internal separation is needed without introducing a full section break.

### **Supported UI blocks for the** **[Content area](/sites/construct/divided-block#anatomy)**

1.  **Description / body text** - Use for long-form descriptions or explanations, when full sentences and paragraphs work best.
2.  **Image** - Include a 3:2 or 16:9 ratio image to illustrate or enhance the content including: photography, isometric illustrations, diagrams, and similar visuals. The image can include an optional caption.
3.  **Video** - Embed and link video content in 16:9 ratio.
4.  **[List](/global/construct/list)** - List up to 10 items, with bullet type options including: none, bullet, status, or number.
5.  **Code block** - Display preformatted code.
6.  **[Logo block](/sites/construct/logo-block)** - Display related logos such as customers or partners.
7.  **[Linked logo block](/sites/construct/linked-logo-block)** - Display logos that link to additional resources.
8.  **[CTA block](/sites/construct/cta-block)** - Share one or more call-to-action elements, including a primary button, secondary button, and link.
9.  **[Notification](/global/construct/notification)** - Highlight alerts, updates, or contextual messages within the section.

### When to use

*   Each item represents a single step, stage, or content item within a broader topic
*   Items need clear visual separation while remaining part of the same section
*   Each item may contain a different combination of content types
*   Content requires richer structure than a simple heading and body

### When not to use

*   All blocks share the same content structure — use [Basic section](/sites/construct/basic-section) without a dividing rule instead
*   The image is the primary content with only brief supporting text — use an [Equal height block](/sites/construct/equal-height-block) instead
*   Content is purely text-based — use [Tiered list](/sites/construct/tiered-list) instead

table.name: Rule
table.description: The muted rule component indicates the beginning of a new block. It is not used for the first block and appears from the second block onward.

table.name: Heading bullet
table.description: a visual marker that precedes the heading. Supports three options: bullet, icon, or number. When present, indentation is applied to the heading and content area below it.

table.name: Heading
table.description: a short title that identifies the step, stage, or content item, rendered as H5

table.name: Content area
table.description: the main body of the block, supporting text, images, videos, lists, code blocks, logos, notifications, and CTA block.

properties.name: Block rule
properties.description: The rule component to separate each  block

properties.name: Heading
properties.description: 4-column width H5 heading for divided block

properties.name: Bullet style
properties.description: The bullet style defines how the block heading is visually presented within each divided block. When the bullet option is used, an additional 32px indent is applied to the content area below the heading to align the content visually with the bullet marker.

properties.name: Block item bottom padding
properties.description: The type of padding applied to the block item
properties.constraint: The last block item of the pattern/block must not have bottom padding.

properties.name: Image
properties.description: The container for placing visual content such as images, illustrations, or diagrams.

properties.name: Image aspect ratio
properties.description: Indicate ratio of the image

properties.name: Image caption
properties.description: Optional HTML caption below the image

properties.name: Image responsive aspect ratio
properties.description: Change the aspect ratio of an image container, depending on the screen size

properties.name: Object fit
properties.description: Defines how the image fill its container

properties.name: Primary CTA
properties.description: The CTA used with the positive button style. It indicates a high-priority, positive action and is reserved for hero areas or other key call-to-action sections.

properties.name: Secondary CTA
properties.description: The CTA using the default button style. It is used for most actions that are positive but not primary, and may serve as the main CTA in non-hero sections.

properties.name: Link CTA
properties.description: The text link used for contextual actions or pathways to more information. It uses a chevron (›) to indicate navigation to additional content or pages.

properties.name: Text
properties.description: The code that is being shown inside of the code component.

properties.name: Type
properties.description: What semantic type the notification is.

properties.name: Border
properties.description: Wether the notification has a border or not. Borderless notifications are used for example in [Table](wiki-ref://grid-ymtdOF6n4R/i-aUPUyjNgjD).

properties.name: Inline
properties.description: Wether the title and description of the notification are being shown in one line. reduces the overall height of the notification allowing it to be used where space is at a premium.

properties.name: Title
properties.description: Sets the content of the title.

properties.name: Description
properties.description: Sets the content of the description.

properties.name: Dismissible
properties.description: Wether or not the notification can be dismissed. If it can be dismissed the dismiss button is being shown.

properties.name: Actions
properties.description: Allows to give associated actions with the notification.

properties.name: Time stamp
properties.description: Can be set to the timestamp when the event when that triggered the notification took place.

properties.name: Logo block
properties.description: The logo block showcases a group of related images, such as a group of customer or partner logos.
properties.constraint:
- Avoid exceeding 10 logos 
- Linking individual logos is not allowed

properties.name: List
properties.description: The grouping of multiple list items.

properties.name: Orientation
properties.description: Whether the list is displayed horizontally or vertically.

properties.name: Text
properties.description: Sets the content of the list

properties.name: Marker
properties.description: Decorator for a single list item.
properties.constraint: All items in the same level of nesting must have the same marker type. No marker allowed when using dots to separate horizontal lists.

properties.name: Divider (vertical list)
properties.description: Whether has muted rule between list items

properties.name: Divider (horizontal list)
properties.description: Whether the list has vertical line as a separator or a dot.

properties.name: Nesting level
properties.description: Hierarchical list structures
